Q1:
Information about the Research and Consent Form

Study title: Comparing Explanation Types in a Healthcare Symptom Chatbot
Introduction

You are invited to take part in a research project that is part of an artificial intelligence bachelor's degree thesis at Radboud University. Before you decide if you want to take part in this study, it is necessary for you to have enough information about the nature of the study, the tasks which you will be required to perform and how your data will be used.

Who is conducting the study?
 This study is conducted by [Faisal Ziai], Bachelor Artificial Intelligence student at Radboud University.

Supervisor: [Marcos de Paula Bueno]

Researcher contact: [Faisal.Ziai@ru.nl].

What are we investigating?

This study looks at how people perceive various ways a healthcare symptom chatbot explains things. The chatbot is not an actual diagnostic or medical tool. Instead, the chatbot uses pre-written explanations for a set of specific, low-risk symptom-based questions (e.g. headache, stomach ache, cold symptoms, etc.).

Each symptom-based question has the same potential "medical" outcome. However, the explanation provided to explain what might cause a person's symptoms varies. The goal is to determine whether one style of explanation is perceived as better than another in terms of being clear, trustworthy, helpful, satisfying, and easy to use.

What does participation involve?
 To participate, you would need to complete an online study with a customized version of GPT chatbot, and a Qualtrics questionnaire. In addition, you will need to select 20 of the 30 predetermined symptom questions. The chatbot will provide two versions of explanations for each selected symptom-based question. The questions and the gpt-link are send to you beforehand. It is recommended to use the gpt trough your laptop and fill in the qualtrics survery trough your phone. Another possibility is to use the gpt and not delete the tab of the gpt, so you can still access and see which expanations you received. Every explanation has a code like (H3A) or (B4E), these are the explanations codes so you can access and find the explanations faster. Then you will rate both explanations relative to their clarity, trustworthiness, usefulness, satisfaction level and ease-of-use. Finally, you will state which of the explanations you received you prefer overall. The total amount of time required to complete this study will be approximately 15-20 minutes. All responses are subjective, therefore there are no correct or incorrect answers. Answer the study questions as honestly as you see fit based on your personal experiences and opinions.


	
		
			Important: This study is not medical advice. The chatbot cannot diagnose you and should not be used for real health decisions. If you have real health concerns, please contact a general practitioner or urgent medical care. If you think it is an emergency, contact emergency services.
		
	

  

Compensation
 There is no compensation. Your participation helps with a Bachelor thesis project and contributes to research on explainable AI and healthcare chatbots.

Risks and inconvenience

There is a very small amount of risk associated with participation in this study. You will simply read some brief descriptions of symptoms and potential explanations for those symptoms. A few of the symptoms described in the chatbot are common health complaints that many people have experienced themselves. Therefore they may elicit a slight sense of unease. You may withdraw from the study at any point prior to completing the questionnaire.

Both the chatbot and the survey are designed specifically for research purposes. Your input will not be evaluated for clinical or medically related content. Additionally, there will be no medical feedback.

Who should not participate?
 You should not participate if you are under 18 years old, unless the researcher has arranged separate approval and consent procedures. You should also not participate if reading common health symptom scenarios would make you uncomfortable, or if you are looking for real medical advice.

What data will we collect and how will the data be handled?
 We will collect only enough information during the study to answer our research question. We do not request your name, street address, telephone number or e-mail address within the questionnaire. Our intention is to create completely anonymous data. Although we do not plan to disable or remove technical identifiers such as IP addresses generated by the survey platform by default, they will not be analyzed.


	
		
			Type of data
			Purpose
			How are data stored?
			How long are data stored?
			Shared after the study?
		
		
			Research data: age category, education level, AI familiarity, healthcare background, selected question codes, explanation survey codes, 1-7 ratings, preference choices, and optional open-text comments.
			To answer the research question about how participants evaluate different explanation types in a healthcare chatbot.
			Anonymous research data. Data are stored securely according to Radboud University guidelines.
			At least 10 years, in line with Radboud University research data retention guidelines.
			Anonymous data may be shared for research transparency, thesis assessment, or open science purposes.
		
		
			Online consent response and timestamp, if recorded by Qualtrics.
			To show that participants actively agreed to participate before starting the study.
			Stored with the anonymous survey response. No name or handwritten signature is collected.

			

			

			

			

			

			

			

			

			

			

			 
			At least 10 years, unless the final Data Management Plan states otherwise.
			No directly identifiable consent data will be shared.
		
	


Sharing of research data

Anonymous research data may potentially be released in order to allow other researchers to view or understand the findings of our research. When we refer to "anonymous", we mean that your data cannot be traced back to you. Any reports regarding the results of our research will occur at an aggregate level rather than at an individual participant level.

Voluntary participation and withdrawal
 Your decision to participate in this study is voluntary. At any point prior to submitting your completed questionnaire, you can withdraw by closing your web browser. You do not need to provide a justification for withdrawing from the study. Since the questionnaire is anonymous, it may not be feasible to delete your individual entry once you submit it to us unless a separate method for providing a withdrawal code is established. If you wish to utilize withdrawal codes, please describe that process above.

Questions and contact
 If you have questions about the study, please contact [Faisal Ziai ] at [Faisal.Ziai@ru.nl].

For questions about the processing of data in this study, you may contact privacy.fsw@ru.nl.

If you have questions about your rights as a participant, you may contact the Ethics Committee Social Sciences (ECSS) at ecsw@ru.nl.

Ethical review
 This study has will be submitted for ethical review under the relevant Radboud University procedure.
